channel.title: Stockwatch - Key Events
channel.description: Real-time corporate announcements, deals, and financial results.

item.title: [5PAISA] 5paisa Capital Grants 50,000 ESOPs to Employees
item.description:
5paisa Capital Ltd's Nomination & Remuneration Committee approved the grant of 50,000 stock options to identified employees under the "5Paisa Employee Stock Option Scheme – 2023" on April 30, 2026. Each option is convertible into one fully paid-up equity share with a face value of ₹10, and the exercise price is ₹10 per option. The options will vest according to a schedule approved by the NRC, with a minimum one-year period between the grant and vesting. Vested options can be exercised by eligible employees within three years from the vesting date. The scheme is governed and administered by the NRC, which determines the number and nature of options granted based on employee level.

  • 5paisa Capital granted 50,000 stock options to employees.
  • Options are under the "5Paisa ESOS – 2023" scheme.
  • Exercise price is ₹10 per option, convertible to one equity share.
  • Vesting schedule determined by NRC, with a minimum one-year period.
  • Vested options can be exercised within three years from vesting date.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: 5paisa Capital Ltd | Category: ESOP

item.title: [EVEREADY] Eveready Q4 FY26 Revenue up 9.4%, EBITDA up 10.7%; FY26 Revenue up 8.2%
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Eveready Industries India Ltd reported robust growth for Q4 FY26 and the full financial year ended March 31, 2026. In Q4 FY26, consolidated revenue from operations increased by 9.4% to ₹327.2 crore, driven by strong performance in batteries (Alkaline up 82%) and lighting (up 17.0%). EBITDA rose 10.7% to ₹28.7 crore, supported by improved realizations and cost management. Profit after tax was ₹141.8 crore, including an exceptional gain of ₹102.7 crore. For FY26, consolidated revenue grew 8.2% to ₹1455.4 crore, with an EBITDA margin of 11.5%. PAT for FY26 was ₹171.5 crore, including a net exceptional gain of ₹48.6 crore. The company strengthened its market share, with alkaline batteries approaching 20% and dry cell batteries maintaining over 52%. A key strategic milestone was the commissioning of India’s only operational Alkaline battery facility in Jammu, enhancing manufacturing scale and reducing import reliance. The company also reduced debt by over ₹100 crore in the fiscal year.

  • Q4 FY26 revenue grew 9.4% to ₹327.2 crore; EBITDA rose 10.7% to ₹28.7 crore.
  • FY26 consolidated revenue increased 8.2% to ₹1455.4 crore.
  • PAT for Q4 FY26 was ₹141.8 crore, including ₹102.7 crore exceptional gain.
  • Commissioned India's only operational Alkaline battery facility in Jammu.
  • Company reduced debt by over ₹100 crore in the current fiscal year.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: EVEREADY INDUSTRIES INDIA LTD. | Category: Quarterly Result

item.title: [LAURUSLABS] Laurus Labs Appoints Two New Independent Directors, One Retires
item.description:
Laurus Labs Ltd's Board of Directors, at their meeting on April 30, 2026, approved the appointment of Dr. Shekhar Chintamani Mande and Ms. Sutapa Banerjee as Independent Directors. Both appointments are for a term of five consecutive years, effective from July 02, 2026, and are subject to shareholder approval. Dr. Mande is a distinguished structural and computational biologist and former Director General of CSIR. Ms. Banerjee's profile was also annexed. The Board also noted the retirement of Mrs. Aruna Bhinge as an Independent Director, effective from the close of business hours on July 6, 2026, upon completion of her second term. The new appointees are confirmed to be unrelated to any existing directors or Key Managerial Personnel and are not debarred by any regulatory authority.

  • Laurus Labs' Board approved two new Independent Directors.
  • Dr. Shekhar Chintamani Mande and Ms. Sutapa Banerjee appointed for 5 years.
  • Appointments are effective July 2, 2026, pending shareholder approval.
  • Mrs. Aruna Bhinge will retire as Independent Director on July 6, 2026.
  • New directors are independent and not related to company management.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Laurus Labs Ltd | Category: Management Change

item.title: [GEMSI] Gemstone Investments converts 8.28 Cr warrants into equity shares for ₹20.7 Cr
item.description:
Gemstone Investments Ltd's Board of Directors approved the conversion of 8,28,00,000 convertible warrants into an equal number of equity shares on April 30, 2026. Each warrant was converted into one equity share of face value ₹1/- at a conversion price of ₹2.50/- per share, upon receipt of the full subscription amount. This preferential allotment to non-promoter investors aggregates to ₹20,70,00,000. The conversion was carried out in compliance with SEBI ICDR Regulations and the Companies Act, 2013. Key allottees include Maniben Chandrakant Shah, Manisha Mukesh Gala, and SP and Nisha Private Limited.

  • Company converted 8.28 crore convertible warrants into equity shares.
  • Each warrant converted into one equity share at ₹2.50 per share.
  • Total capital raised through this conversion is ₹20.70 crore.
  • Shares were allotted to non-promoter investors via preferential allotment.
  • This action increases the company's equity base and capital.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: GEMSTONE INVESTMENTS LTD. | Category: Corporate Action

item.title: [SONACOMS] Sona BLW Board approves Q4/FY26 results, ₹1.80 dividend, $6M Mexico investment
item.description:
Sona BLW Precision Forgings Ltd's Board of Directors approved audited standalone and consolidated financial results for the quarter and financial year ended March 31, 2026. The Board recommended a final dividend of ₹1.80 per equity share for FY 2025-26, fixing June 26, 2026, as the record date for eligibility. Additionally, the Board approved a USD 6 million investment in its wholly-owned subsidiary, SONA BLW eDRIVE MEXICANA, S.A.P.I. DE C.V., to support capex, operational expenditure, and working capital. The purpose of a previously approved corporate guarantee of up to USD 10 million for the Mexican subsidiary was modified to include securing both fund-based and non-fund-based borrowing facilities. Lastly, the Board ratified a change in the shareholding percentage for the Chennai captive solar power plant investment from 26% to 27.64%, while the investment amount of ₹8.30 million remains unchanged.

  • Board approved Q4 and FY26 audited financial results.
  • Recommended ₹1.80 final dividend for FY26; record date June 26, 2026.
  • Approved USD 6 million investment in Mexican subsidiary.
  • Modified purpose of USD 10 million corporate guarantee for Mexico unit.
  • Adjusted shareholding for Chennai captive solar plant investment.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Sona BLW Precision Forgings Ltd | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [TARINI] Tarini International FY26 Results: Auditor Issues Qualified Opinion
item.description:
Tarini International Ltd's Board approved its Audited Standalone and Consolidated Financial Results for the half and financial year ended March 31, 2026. The auditors, M. Modi & Associates, issued a qualified opinion. The qualification is primarily due to the company's investment of ₹121.59 Lakhs in loss-making subsidiaries, for which no provision for diminution was made. The auditors noted that if this provision had been made, the profit before tax would convert into a loss of ₹43.20 Lakhs. Additionally, the auditors emphasized unconfirmed receivables and ongoing legal matters, including an Enforcement Directorate attachment on a company asset and an appeal against a ₹505 Lakhs SEBI penalty.

  • Board approved Audited Standalone and Consolidated Financial Results for FY2026.
  • Auditors issued a qualified opinion on the financial results.
  • Qualification due to unprovided diminution in investment in loss-making subsidiaries (₹121.59 Lakhs).
  • Potential conversion of profit to ₹43.20 Lakhs loss if provision was made.
  • Unconfirmed receivables and ongoing legal cases (ED attachment, SEBI penalty appeal) noted.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Tarini International Ltd | Category: Quarterly Result

item.title: [URJAGLOBA] Urja Global CEO Sushil Dubey Resigns Effective April 30, 2026
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Urja Global Ltd. has announced the resignation of Mr. Sushil Dubey from the position of Chief Executive Officer (CEO). His resignation is effective from the close of business hours on April 30, 2026. The company stated that Mr. Dubey cited personal reasons and other professional engagements for his departure. He has confirmed that there are no other material reasons for his resignation beyond those mentioned. This disclosure was made in accordance with Regulation 30 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015.

  • Urja Global's CEO, Mr. Sushil Dubey, has resigned.
  • His resignation is effective from April 30, 2026.
  • Reasons cited are personal and other professional engagements.
  • No other material reasons for the resignation were disclosed.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: URJA GLOBAL LTD. | Category: Management Change

item.title: [GEMSI] Gemstone Investments converts 8.28 Cr warrants to equity, raises ₹20.70 Cr
item.description:
Gemstone Investments Ltd's Board approved the conversion of 8,28,00,000 convertible warrants into an equal number of equity shares. These shares, with a face value of ₹1 each, were allotted to non-promoters at a conversion price of ₹2.50 per share, following a preferential allotment. The company received the full subscription amount, totaling ₹20,70,00,000. This action is in accordance with SEBI ICDR Regulations and the Companies Act, 2013.

  • 8.28 crore convertible warrants converted into equity shares.
  • Shares allotted to non-promoters via preferential allotment.
  • Conversion price set at ₹2.50 per equity share.
  • Company raised ₹20.70 crore from the conversion.
  • Each warrant converted into one equity share of ₹1 face value.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: GEMSTONE INVESTMENTS LTD. | Category: Investment

item.title: [TARINI] Tarini International FY26 Results: Auditor Issues Qualified Opinion
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Tarini International Ltd's Board approved its audited standalone and consolidated financial results for the half and financial year ended March 31, 2026. The independent auditor issued a qualified opinion, primarily due to the management's assumption of going concern for subsidiaries with accumulated losses and net capital deficiency, without making a provision for diminution in investment of ₹121.59 Lakhs. The auditor noted that if this provision were made, the company's profit before tax would convert into a loss of ₹43.20 Lakhs. The report also included an emphasis of matter regarding unconfirmed receivables, a provisionally attached farm house by the Enforcement Directorate, and a pending Supreme Court appeal against a SEBI penalty of ₹505 Lakhs.

  • Board approved audited standalone and consolidated financial results for FY2026.
  • Auditor issued a qualified opinion on the financial results.
  • Qualified opinion due to lack of provision for ₹121.59 Lakhs diminution in subsidiary investments.
  • Unprovided amount would convert profit to a ₹43.20 Lakhs loss.
  • Emphasis on unconfirmed receivables, attached asset, and pending SEBI penalty appeal.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Tarini International Ltd | Category: Quarterly Updates

item.title: [INDIAMART] IndiaMART approves FY26 results, recommends ₹60/share dividend
item.description:
IndiaMART InterMESH Ltd's Board of Directors approved the Audited Consolidated and Standalone Financial Results for the quarter and financial year ended March 31, 2026. The board also recommended a final dividend of ₹30/- per equity share and a special dividend of ₹30/- per equity share, totaling ₹60/- per share for FY 2025-26, subject to shareholder approval. The Record Date for this dividend has been fixed as Friday, June 19, 2026. Additionally, the 27th Annual General Meeting (AGM) is scheduled for Monday, June 29, 2026. The financial results are accompanied by an Auditors' Report with an unmodified opinion.

  • Board approved audited Q4 and FY26 financial results.
  • Recommended total dividend of ₹60 per equity share for FY26.
  • Dividend record date fixed as June 19, 2026.
  • 27th Annual General Meeting scheduled for June 29, 2026.
  • Auditors issued an unmodified opinion on financial results.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: IndiaMART InterMESH Ltd | Category: Quarterly Result
